channel.title: NPR Topics: News
channel.description: NPR news, audio, and podcasts. Coverage of breaking stories, national and world news, politics, business, science, technology, and extended coverage of major national and world events.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: Kneecap is used to controversy. On new album 'Fenian,' they're leaning further in
item.description: NPR's Juana Summers talks with Mo Chara and Móglaí Bap of the Irish hip-hop trio Kneecap about their new album Fenian.
item.content:encoded:
NPR's Juana Summers talks with Mo Chara and Móglaí Bap of the Irish hip-hop trio Kneecap about their new album Fenian.

(Image credit: Yamil Lage/AFP via Getty Images)
item.dc:creator: Kathryn Fink

item.title: Several states — and the LA public schools — are setting limits on screen time
item.description: Four states have recently passed legislation to limit teaching and assessments via screens for students. So has the United States' second-largest school district.
item.content:encoded: Four states have recently passed legislation to limit teaching and assessments via screens for students. So has the United States' second-largest school district.
item.dc:creator: Sequoia Carrillo

item.title: The expert on 'super aging' breaks down the science — and grift — in anti-aging
item.description: From peptides and protein to sleep hygiene and vaccines, what actually helps you age well? Physician Eric Topol breaks down the science — and the myths — of longevity and anti-aging.
item.content:encoded:
From peptides and protein to sleep hygiene and vaccines, what actually helps you age well? Physician Eric Topol breaks down the science — and the myths — of longevity and anti-aging.

(Image credit: Capuski)
item.dc:creator: Manoush Zomorodi

item.title: Record-breaking DHS shutdown ends. And, May Day protests to draw crowds nationwide
item.description: Congress has ended the record-breaking shutdown of the Department of Homeland Security. And, May Day demonstrations across the U.S. are expected to draw crowds protesting the Trump administration.
item.content:encoded:
Congress has ended the record-breaking shutdown of the Department of Homeland Security. And, May Day demonstrations across the U.S. are expected to draw crowds protesting the Trump administration.

(Image credit: Graeme Sloan)
item.dc:creator: Brittney Melton

item.title: How well can EVs handle the heat — and the cold? AAA put them to the test
item.description: Electric vehicles lose some range in the winter — and, to a lesser degree, in the summer. But exactly how much? AAA has brand-new data.
item.content:encoded:
Electric vehicles lose some range in the winter — and, to a lesser degree, in the summer. But exactly how much? AAA has brand-new data.

(Image credit: Justin Sullivan/Getty Images)
item.dc:creator: Camila Domonoske

item.title: A tech worker in China is laid off and replaced by AI. Is it legal?
item.description: A tech worker in eastern China's Hangzhou city was dismissed after his job was replaced by AI. An appeals court in the city has ruled the dismissal unlawful.
item.content:encoded:
A tech worker in eastern China's Hangzhou city was dismissed after his job was replaced by AI. An appeals court in the city has ruled the dismissal unlawful.

(Image credit: Andy Wong)
item.dc:creator: Jennifer Pak

item.title: Fill in the blank for the quiz: The Trump admin took aim at _____ this week
item.description: This week, the federal government's been busy. There are paint jobs, fresh indictments, commemorative items and more. If you've been paying attention — good job!
item.content:encoded: This week, the federal government's been busy. There are paint jobs, fresh indictments, commemorative items and more. If you've been paying attention — good job!
item.dc:creator: Holly J. Morris

item.title: Taking a look at looksmaxxing — and what parents should know
item.description: The trend among boys and young men of optimizing their physical appearance includes dangerous practices. Experts offer advice on how to talk to their sons about body image and healthy behaviors.
item.content:encoded:
The trend among boys and young men of optimizing their physical appearance includes dangerous practices. Experts offer advice on how to talk to their sons about body image and healthy behaviors. 

(Image credit: Malte Mueller)
item.dc:creator: Alana Wise

item.title: It's Day 1 of Medicaid work requirements in Nebraska. People are worried
item.description: Starting May 1, many people covered by Medicaid in Nebraska have to prove they are working. It's a requirement most states will have to implement under President Trump's budget law, beginning in January.
item.content:encoded:
Starting May 1, many people covered by Medicaid in Nebraska have to prove they are working. It's a requirement most states will have to implement under President Trump's budget law, beginning in January.

(Image credit: Julia Demaree Nikhinson)
item.dc:creator: Phil Galewitz

item.title: Passenger railroads see a sharp jump in ridership as gas prices climb
item.description: More Americans are turning to the train as gas prices reach their highest point since the war in Iran began. Brightline, the privately run railroad in Florida, had its best month ever in March.
item.content:encoded:
More Americans are turning to the train as gas prices reach their highest point since the war in Iran began. Brightline, the privately run railroad in Florida, had its best month ever in March.

(Image credit: Anna Moneymaker)
item.dc:creator: Joel Rose
